leadText: The Friend Ball (Japanese: フレンドボール Friend Ball), also referred to as the Friendship Ball, is a type of Poké Ball introduced in Generation II. It can be used to catch a wild Pokémon. Pokémon caught in a Friend Ball become more friendly. It is one of Kurt's signature Apricorn Poké Balls, and is made from Green Apricorns.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Manual activation [ edit | edit source ] When used from the Bag in a wild encounter, it attempts to catch the wild Pokémon with a catch rate modifier of 1×. If caught, the Pokémon's friendship is set to 200 in Generations II through VII , or 150 in Generation VIII [ 1 ] onwards. It has no effect on the friendship of Pokémon hatched in a Friend Ball. If used on an Ultra Beast , the catch rate modifier is instead always set to 410/4096× (~0.1×). The Friend Ball cannot be used in situations in which Poké Balls cannot be used, such as in wild battles with two or more opponents currently present or against a trial Pokémon. If used in a Trainer battle, the opposing Trainer will bat the ball away, wasting the turn (as well as the ball prior to Generation IV). Held item [ edit | edit source ] Fling fails if the user is holding a Friend Ball. In Pokémon HeartGold and SoulSilver only, Friend Balls cannot be held. This is to prevent them being held by a Pokémon traded to Pokémon Diamond, Pearl , and Platinum , where the item does not exist. ↑ https://twitter.com/Sibuna_Switch/status/1571190686996652034

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description G S C A Ball that makes Pok é mon friendly. HG SS B W B2 W2 A Poké Ball that makes caught Pokémon more friendly. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA A strange Poké Ball that will make the wild Pokémon caught with it more friendly toward you immediately. S V ZA A somewhat different Poké Ball that makes a wild Pokémon more friendly toward you immediately after it's caught.

If caught, the Pokémon's friendship is set to 200 in Generations II through VII , or 150 in Generation VIII [ 1 ] onwards. It has no effect on the friendship of Pokémon hatched in a Friend Ball. If used on an Ultra Beast , the catch rate modifier is instead always set to 410/4096× (~0.1×). The Friend Ball cannot be used in situations in which Poké Balls cannot be used, such as in wild battles with two or more opponents currently present or against a trial Pokémon. Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Finite methods Repeatable methods G S C Azalea Town ( Grn Apricorn ) HG SS Azalea Town ( Grn Apricorn ) S M US UM Heahea City US UM , Malie City Sw Sh Turffield Stadium Event Reward from Ball Guy for clearing Champion Cup tournament during post-game (0.125% chance) Sw Sh IA Cram-o-matic ( Green Apricorn ) Sw Sh CT Reward from Ball Guy for clearing Galarian Star Tournament during post-game (0.125% chance) BD SP Event S V Reward for registering 260 Pokémon in the Paldea Pokédex Porto Marinada auction Academy Ace Tournament S V TM Reward for registering 160 Pokémon in the Kitakami Pokédex S V ID Item Printer (Poké Ball Lotto) ZA Infinite Z-A Royale ZA MD Reward for completing Side Mission 161: "Frigibax's Friend-Finding" Can be given out by Ladies in Hyperspace Lumiose battle zones after defeating them Destroy Floating Poké Ball in 4 Star and 5 Star Hyperspace Zones with Item Power: Poké Balls Distribution [ edit | edit source ] Games Event Language/Region Distribution period S M Global mission items All February 21 to March 31, 2017 Sw Sh Poké Times rare Poké Balls All December 20, 2019 to January 15, 2020 The Isle of Armor promotion packs All May 29 to June 4, 2020 Japanese Pokemon Scraps 2020 Japanese November 20, 2020 to April 30, 2021 Pokémon Adventure 25 items South Korea April 26 to September 30, 2021 BD SP Apricorn Poké Ball Set All November 19, 2021 to February 28, 2022 S V Nintendo Direct Helpful Items All June 21 to July 31, 2023 NPC usage [ edit | edit source ] A Friend Ball in a display case at the Poké Ball Boutique Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Breeders keep their Pokémon in Friend Balls. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Rintaro keeps his Pokémon in Friend Balls. Cameo appearances [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon X and Y [ edit | edit source ] Despite being unobtainable as an item in Generation VI , a Friend Ball appears in the Poké Ball Boutique in Lumiose City . ↑ https://twitter.com/Sibuna_Switch/status/1571190686996652034

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The promo naming error A Kids' WB promo that aired during the fourth season showcasing the various Apricorn Poké Balls incorrectly referred to the Friend Ball as the "Stun Ball."
